message:
perf(fuse): move ioctl trace probe I/O onto a dedicated writer thread (bd-adke)

The previous IoctlTraceProbe wrapped a BufWriter<File> in a Mutex and
performed the writeln! synchronously on the FUSE dispatcher thread on every
ioctl callback. Even with the 64 KiB BufWriter, the per-event lock
contention plus the eventual append/flush syscall path blocked dispatch
under load — exactly what bd-adke flagged.

Replace the Mutex<IoctlTraceProbe> with a bounded mpsc::sync_channel and a
dedicated `ffs-ioctl-trace` writer thread:

  - record(...) is now a non-blocking try_send; on backpressure it bumps
    a Relaxed AtomicU64 drop counter that is surfaced as a `warn!` on
    Drop so the trace's lossiness is auditable.
  - The writer thread owns the File directly (no BufWriter — for a
    diagnostic this low-volume, the kernel page cache is buffer enough,
    and skipping the user-space buffer means external readers see events
    as soon as the writer is scheduled).
  - flush_sync() round-trips a Flush message through the channel,
    providing a deterministic happens-before barrier for tests (the
    writer drains all preceding Records before signaling).
  - Drop closes the channel by taking the sender, joins the writer, and
    logs the drop count if non-zero.

Net effect on the dispatcher path: a single try_send (bounded queue,
4096-deep) instead of a Mutex acquire + writeln! + (eventually) a write
syscall. Field type narrows from Option<Mutex<IoctlTraceProbe>> to
Option<IoctlTraceProbe> since the probe is now internally Send+Sync.

Also adds ioctl_trace_flush_sync_is_happens_before_barrier_for_concurrent_recorders
which spawns 8 threads × 32 records and asserts every event lands in the
file by the time flush_sync returns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
